canonical_clusters.title: Negated or non-current red-flag text can lock a conversation into emergency-only responses

proof_tuple.source: Anonymous consultation text contains a denial, resolved history, quotation, hypothetical, or other non-current mention of a lexical red flag.
proof_tuple.control: EmergencyTriage applies context-free matcher.find() patterns to every concatenated user turn.
proof_tuple.sink: Any match returns the server-authored 119 response before retrieval or model processing; retained history can repeat the short circuit on later turns.
proof_tuple.impact: Ordinary care information can remain unavailable and the user can be repeatedly directed to unnecessary emergency-service use.

canonical_clusters.remediation_boundary: Use a server-owned, clinically reviewed context decision that preserves affirmative and mixed-clause escalation while distinguishing clearly non-affirmative context; do not delegate the decision to the model or screen only the newest turn.
canonical_clusters.rationale: Emergency-context interpretation is a distinct server-owned control; fixes to omitted affirmative patterns or generic rescue copy do not address context-driven false escalation.
counterevidence: The target explicitly prefers false positives because a false negative can be much more harmful.

independent_approval.decision_reason: The sealed independent novel-residue audit confirms strict non-subsumption and the review rejects every same-round merge.

canonical_clusters.title: Unreviewed in-code form qualifiers collapse into exact blocking identity

proof_tuple.source: Government ingredient text or user input contains a token in the in-code FORM_QUALIFIERS set.
proof_tuple.control: Qualifier stripping runs outside the human-signed synonym metadata before identity classification.
proof_tuple.sink: The collapsed value compares as EXACT and AllergyChecker can author BLOCKED.
proof_tuple.impact: A clinically invalid equivalence can create a false high-authority allergy block; an incorrect omission can leave a real equivalence unmatched.

canonical_clusters.remediation_boundary: Put qualifier equivalence behind human-reviewed, source-backed metadata or retain transformation provenance so an unreviewed qualifier cannot independently yield EXACT or BLOCKED; use WARNING or UNKNOWN until review is complete.
canonical_clusters.rationale: FORM_QUALIFIERS authority remains outside the human-signed synonym boundary; fixing signed synonym handling does not remove this in-code exact/blocking path.
counterevidence: The code documents concrete false-negative reasons for physical-form stripping and deliberately excludes salts.
The worker probe proves EXACT/BLOCKED behavior, not clinical incorrectness of any current qualifier.

independent_approval.decision_reason: The sealed independent novel-residue audit confirms strict non-subsumption and the review rejects every same-round merge.

canonical_clusters.title: Outer malformed chat JSON reaches the catch-all logger with attacker health text

proof_tuple.source: Anonymous chat sends malformed JSON whose unquoted token contains symptom or identifying text.
proof_tuple.control: HttpMessageNotReadableException is absent from the sanitized invalid-request handler.
proof_tuple.sink: The outer catch-all logs the full Spring/Jackson exception and cause, including the attacker token.
proof_tuple.impact: Health-text fragments can persist in application, container, or aggregated logs and widen access to consultation data.

canonical_clusters.remediation_boundary: Classify HttpMessageNotReadableException in the outer handler and log only value-free structured metadata, or otherwise prevent parser messages and Throwable stacks from reaching logs.
canonical_clusters.rationale: The outer malformed-body handler is independently reachable; remediating the nested-worktree handler in R01-CAN-066 does not change this log sink.
counterevidence: The client receives a generic INTERNAL_ERROR response; this protects the response only, not server-side log persistence.

taxonomy_variants.category: CWE-532 / sensitive information in log files
